entry.title: Re: Automatic Account Determination - Value String - GOOD...
entry.content:
Why BSX/WRX are triggered because Movement type 101 Q (GR for project stock) is technically a variant of the standard 101 movement. Even though OMJJ shows value string WE06, the system often defaults to WE01 if:

  • the purchase order is not account-assigned properly (e.g., missing project assignment or wrong category).
  • the configuration in OME9 for Account Assignment Category "Q" doesn’t fully align with the GR posting logic.
  • the valuation class and account determination in OBYC are not mapped for the WE06 transaction/event keys.

So, if the system cannot find valid accounts for WE06, it falls back to the standard GR/IR clearing (WRX) and inventory posting (BSX).

Having said that, the system is not “wrong,” it’s just defaulting because your PO/account assignment setup is not fully aligned with project stock logic. Fixing the PO account assignment and OBYC mappings should make WE06 kick in.

entry.title: Re: Copy control from Delivery type LF to Billing type F2...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you cannot manually add new entries to copy control (LF → F2) because customizing is locked under the “fit-to-standard” approach. To invoice item category CBTA (MTO Standard Item), you must use the standard extensibility options: either assign CBTA to an existing copy control mapping delivered by SAP, or raise a request via SAP Influence/Customer Care to extend the scope.

For information, below is a comparison chart

entry.title: Flexible Workflow Email Notifications – Standard or Custom?
entry.content:
Flexible Workflow has been implemented in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, and there is a requirement to configure email notifications for specific approval events.

Requirement:

  • On PR/PO rejection, an email should be sent to the requisitioner.
  • In a 5-level approval workflow, once the first approver approves, an email should be triggered to the PO creator and the next-level approver, indicating that the PO is pending for their approval.

Is this achievable through standard configuration, or does it require a custom enhancement (e.g., BAdI/workflow extension)?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.


#FlexibleWorkflow #PrivateCloud

entry.title: Re: Default Cost center and profit center assignemnt for...
entry.content: No not yet available in Public Cloud to my knowledge.  If this functionality is critical, raise an incident to SAP to confirm roadmap delivery and request guidance on interim workarounds.

entry.title: Javascript Date function and dynamic coloring
entry.content:
While working in SAP adobe form, developers come across a situation where they need to calculate the arrears based on the creation date and payment date of a document. Also, there could be some requirements to color a particular cell based on the values populated. With the help of Javascript we can achieve these requirements in on premise system as well as on public cloud. 

In an on premise system the arrear days calculation can be handled using the ABAP code but while working in SAP Public cloud we need to make use of scripting language to achieve such requirements. 
In the below example I have demonstrated to use the Date function in javascript to calculate the arrears. Also the dynamic coloring of cells based on the value populated.

To demonstrate the example I have taken invoices from invoice table DFKKINVDOC_H.

Step 1: Create an adobe form interface in SFP T-code and do the relevant data select. Data can be selected as per the business requirements, but here for the simplicity I have selected all the invoices corresponding to a customer.

 

Step 2: Create the form layout in SFP T-Code and use the interface created in the previous step.

 

Step 3: I have added a column called as Arrears to calculate the date difference between the invoice creation date and net due date. 
Let's first generate the pdf and see the current output. 

Step 4: Now, we need to calculate the arrear days between the dates. We can do it using ABAP also but javascript helps us in public cloud.
To do so, select the Arrears column and then select the Calculate event and also select the Language as Javascript.

Step 5: Let's write the Javascript Code to calculate the difference between the dates using date function.

Now, when we have written the code in javascript, let's generate the pdf now and see if the data is coming for Arrears.

( Please ignore some of the values here as it is a development system and it can have data discrepancies. Our purpose is to use the Date function to calculate the difference )

var creationDate = this.parent.CRDATE.rawValue  // Creation date of the record
var dueDate = this.parent.FAEDN.rawValue;        // Invoice due date
// Split the raw value and get the correct date format. JS has this format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00
var invoiceCrDate = (creationDate.split("T")[0]).split("-");  // -> This will convert date format from YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00 to YYYY-MM-DD
var invoiceDueDate = (dueDate.split("T")[0]).split("-");
// Get the Date, Month and Year individually for invoice Creation Date
var invCrYear = parseInt(invoiceCrDate[0]);
var invCrMonth = parseInt(invoiceCrDate[1]) - 1;  // Javascript Months are 0 index based and hence -1 is required
var invCrDate = parseInt(invoiceCrDate[2]);
// Get the Date, Month and Year individually for invoice Due Date
var invDueYear = parseInt(invoiceDueDate[0]);
var invDueMonth = parseInt(invoiceDueDate[1]) - 1;  // Javascript Months are 0 index based and hence -1 is required
var invDueDate = parseInt(invoiceDueDate[2]);
// Create a new Date object to find the difference
var startDate = new Date(invCrYear, invCrMonth, invCrDate, 00, 00, 00, 00);
var endDate = new Date(invDueYear, invDueMonth, invDueDate, 00, 00, 00, 00);
// Calculate the values of one day
var oneDay = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;  // here multiply with 1000 else the value will come in decimal
// calculate the difference
this.rawValue = Math.round((startDate - endDate) / oneDay);

Let's proceed further to color the cells accordingly. For example, we will color the cell with red if the arrear days is less than 0, color the cell with green if the arrear days is greater than 0, and keep the cell color as it is if the arrear days is equal to 0.

Step 6: First we need to find the RGB value of colors which are going to be used. We can find it easily on the internet. For our case we need RGB value of Red and Green color. 

( Based on your requirement and color combination, you can find the rgb value accordingly )
Red -> rgb(255, 0, 0)
Green -> rgb(0, 255, 0)

Just add the below additional code in the previous code( code written in step 5 )

// Let's assume that the arrears' cell with value greater than 0 should be colored with green color 
// And the cell value less than 0 should be colored with red and for 0 no color
if ( this.rawValue < 0 ){
      this.fillColor = "255,0,0", this.rawValue;
}
else if ( this.rawValue > 0 ){
       this.fillColor = "0,255,0", this.rawValue;
}



Step 7: Now, let's generate the pdf once again and see the output.

With the help of above steps we can achieve our arrear calculation as well as dynamic coloring of cell according to the value. This is one of the use cases of Date function in Javascript, you can have different scenarios to use the date function.

** One small bonus trick in JS, many of you might be knowing but this is also a kind of common requirements. The user may ask the developer to put the symbol of currency dynamically in the amount field **

Step 8 :  Select the Amount field and select the calculate event and then write the below code.

 

// Write the symbol of currency based on the recods.
var currKey = this.parent.TOTAL_CURR.rawValue;
if ( currKey == 'EUR' ){
      this.format.picture.value = "num{'€' z,zzz,zzz,zz9.99}", this.rawValue;
   }
else if ( currKey == 'USD' ){
      this.format.picture.value = "num{'$' z,zzz,zzz,zz9.99}", this.rawValue;
}
else {
      this.format.picture.value = "num{z,zzz,zzz,zz9.99}", this.rawValue;
}

 

Step 9: Let's generate the pdf once again and see the current output.

You may want to minimize the width of Curreny field( in our scenario it is the last column if you intent to show only the amount with symbol ).

Please let know if you have any other solutions for the above mentioned requirements. It will be helpful to all the ABAP developers.

Happy Learning !!!

Thanks,
Shubham

entry.title: Story Reporting – Reporting for SAP SF ONB 2.0. Enable and understand standard onboarding reports
entry.content:
Story Reports with Onboarding:

The Story report type in Report Center is a central part of the People Analytics solution and is based on the integration of the SAP SuccessFactors HCM solution with SAP Analytics Cloud.

A story report is a presentation-style report that uses charts, tables, text, images, and interactive components to display and describe data.

A story report mainly involves building a query and using the query to create the report. We can share our story with others to enable them to use the report you’ve created. It's the newest type of report in the SAP SuccessFactors platform.

Story reports are the recommended report type for all new reports. With story reports, we can:

Search across all schemas, reporting on fields from different schemas that are linked by using auto joins and semi-hard joins

Report on data from product schemas that are not supported by other reporting tools in SAP SuccessFactors, such as:

  • Onboarding (2.0)
  • Job Profile Builder
  • Spot Awards
  • Custom MDF Objects

Use additional calculated functions not available in other SAP SuccessFactors reporting tools (for example, CONTAINS, LENGTH, UPPERCASE).

Characteristics of Story Reports are:

  • Modern visualizations
  • Live SAP SuccessFactors data
  • Intelligent joins across modules
  • Secure, role-based access
  • People-centric data privacy and protection
  • Use data sources: All but Recruiting Marketing, Recruiting Posting, and Onboarding 1.0. However, you can report on data from product schemas that aren't supported by other reporting types in SAP SuccessFactors, such as Onboarding (2.0), Job Profile Builder, and Spot Awards.
  • Create and display ratios
  • Use formats: CSV, PDF, or XLS.

Prerequisites For Enabling story reports for SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.

Reporting Prerequisites: Enable Story Report

  • Onboarding Admin Access
  • Enable Create Story and Share Reports to Groups & Roles permissions.
  • Enable the Create Story permission and select the schemas you want to use while creating report stories.
  • Enable schema-specific permissions to access data from the schema tables. Refer to the Schema Permissions Required for Using Story topic for more information.

Onboarding Prerequisites:

  • Custom Task (Using MDF) is created and available in the system. This is only needed if you want to report on Custom tasks.

Create a Story with Onboarding Data

  • In order to create Story Reports for People Analytics for SAP SuccessFactors, you first need to create a query. The query mines the data from the system that can power the tables and graphs.    
  • Creating a Story Report with Onboarding data is very similar to how a Story Report is created for every other module in SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite. The only difference is the data in the report. It is beneficial to know the different MDF objects for Onboarding to identify the data you can generate on a Story Report.

Steps

  1. To open the Report Center, go to Reporting from the main navigation menu.
  2. To create a new report, choose New.
  3. In the pop-up window, choose Story, then Select.
  4. The Query Designer window opens with a list of Available Data to choose your query from.
  5. Select an object/data token from the Onboarding category. Do this by clicking and dragging the object/data token from the Available Data to the left-hand pane. The left-hand pane is referred to as a canvas where a query is built.

  1. Select specific pieces of data (fields) from the selected object/data token. You can add more objects/data tokens from other schemas (such as SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting), depending on the initial schema chosen. In this case, Onboarding is the initial schema and is integrated with Recruiting.

  1. To save your query (which is your Datasource), choose Finish and enter a Name for it.
  2. Add widgets like Charts and define each as needed. For example, you can select fields to display in the charts.
  3. To save your story, select Save from the File section.

Result

  • You have successfully created a Story Report that contains Onboarding data.

Standard Story Reports for SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

There are 2 standard reports provided as of now and we can add them from success store

here are the steps to add and activate standard reports:

  1. To open the Report Center, go to Reporting from the main navigation menu.
  2. Click on import

      3. Go to content store

 

     4. Search for Onb

      4. Select SF_ONB_I9_E-Verify_Analysis_v2.1 story report and click on import. It takes few                minutes to add story report to system.

      5. Follow the same steps to add SF_ONB_I9_Overdue_Tasks_v2.0 story report. You will find            report under report center once it is added.

I9 E-Verify Analysis
Story : SF_ONB_I9_E-Verify_Analysis_v2.1
Description
Provides all the details about USCIS Form 1-9 and E-Verify overview by showing all the new hires based on different org units and the new hires which are not going through I-9.

Form I-9 is a United States compliance form used by employers to verify individuals’ identity and employment authorization in the United States. Failing to complete a Form I-9 correctly could leave employers subject to civil money penalties.

E-Verify is an online system that helps employers in the United States remotely verify employment eligibility for all its newly hired employees. SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding is integrated with the DHS E-Verify system, providing real-time verification within Onboarding.

The Form I-9 and E-Verify Analysis report provides an overview and details of new hires undergoing US compliance electronically through SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.

The first page of the story shows the following key figures:

  • Total number of new hires
  • Number of new hires not going through the Form I-9 process

In addition, we provide the following charts:

  • Number of new hires according to dimension as organizational units like Legal Entity and Cost Center
  • The Ratio of new hires going through USCIS Form I-9 process to new hires without Form I-9 process
  • New Hires per Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Expiry
  • Number of new hires for whom Form I-9 section 2 is completed within three business days
  • E-Verify case status of new hires

Furthermore, an overview of each new hire such as User ID, Manager name, Department, and Division is displayed in a table.

The second page of the story shows the details of Form I-9 and E-Verify for the new hires.

 
 

I9 Overdue Tasks
Story: SF_ONB_I9_Overdue_Tasks_v2.0
Description
Provides an overview of the I9 overdue task analysis along with the task details per employee.

The Form I-9 Overdue tasks report provides details of Form I-9 Section 2 tasks that are overdue or completed after the due date (within three business days from the start date).

The first page of the story shows the following key figures:

  • Number of new hires with Form I-9 section 2 tasks overdue / completed after the due date
  • Number of Form I-9 section 2 tasks overdue / completed after the due date

It might be relevant to show only the tasks in a particular time range by applying a filter for the ‘task due date’.

In addition, the following charts are provided:

  • New Hires with Form I-9 section 2 tasks overdue / completed after due date according to dimension as organizational units like Legal Entity and Cost Center.
  • Current status of non-compliant Form I-9 section 2 tasks.
  • The tables display the overdue task details for each new: The upper table shows the new hires with their task type, task status, and processor who completed the task. The lower table shows the responsible user details.
  • The second page of the story shows the details of USCIS Form I-9 related Overdue tasks

Summary: 

  • Unlike onboarding 1.0 we don't have all reports available readily. However we can build similar reports with story reports and we encourage all to use story report
  • Story Reports are mandatory, not optional, for effective Onboarding 2.0 reporting.
  • SAP provides a limited but strong compliance reporting baseline, additional insights require custom stories.
  • Investment in People Analytics skills, permissions design, and data modeling is essential.
  • When implemented correctly, Story Reports provide greater flexibility, better governance, and future‑proof reporting compared to legacy tools.

entry.title: Downloading Data in Different Formats Using Factory Design Pattern
entry.content:
Downloading Data in Different Formats Using Factory Design Pattern

In real projects, users often need the ability to download business data (e.g., sales orders, customer data, or flight bookings) in different formats like TXT, PDF, DOC.

If we try to achieve this in a single report with IF/ELSE or CASE statements, the code quickly becomes messy and hard to maintain. Every new requirement (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML) would require changing the program logic.

This is where the Factory Design Pattern helps.

How the Factory Design Pattern Works Here--

  1. Interface (ZIF_FILE_DOWNLOADER)
    1. Defines the common contract: one method DOWNLOAD_FILE.
    2. Ensures all file downloaders behave the same way from the outside.
  1. Concrete Classes (TXT, DOC, PDF, XLSX Downloaders)
    1. Each class implements the interface and contains logic specific to that file type.
    2. Example:
      1. TXT → Uses GUI_DOWNLOAD with ASCII format.
      2. DOC → Uses GUI_DOWNLOAD with linefeeds.
  • PDF → Uses CL_APOC_PDF_GENERATOR and binary download.
  1. XLSX → Uses CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET to generate Excel.
  1. Factory Class (ZCL_FILE_FACTORY)
    1. Acts as a central decision-maker.
    2. Based on user selection (TXT, DOC, PDF, XLSX), it instantiates the correct class.
    3. The main program never directly knows which class it is using — it only calls the common method.
  1. Report Program (ZDEMO6)
    1. Reads user input (p_type).
    2. Calls the factory to get the right downloader object.
    3. Invokes DOWNLOAD_FILE, which internally runs the format-specific logic.

Flow of Execution--

  1. User selects file type on selection screen.
  2. Report calls factory to get the correct downloader.
  3. Factory returns the right class instance (TXT/PDF/DOC/XLSX).
  4. Report calls DOWNLOAD_FILE method (same for all).
  5. File gets saved on the user’s local system in the chosen format.

Benefits of Using Factory Pattern--

Scalability – Easy to add new file formats without modifying existing code.

Maintainability – Each file logic is isolated in its own class.

Reusability – The same design can be reused in multiple applications (ALV exports, reports, integration).

Clean Code – Avoids long IF/ELSE or CASE blocks.

REPORT zdemo6.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* Interface for File Downloaders
*----------------------------------------------------------------------

INTERFACE zif_file_downloader.
  METHODS:
    download_file
      IMPORTING it_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE.
ENDINTERFACE.

CLASS zcl_txt_downloader DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_file_downloader.
ENDCLASS.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* TXT File Downloader Implementation
*----------------------------------------------------------------------


CLASS zcl_txt_downloader IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD zif_file_downloader~download_file.
    DATA: lv_filename TYPE string VALUE 'C:\temp\New folder1'.

    " Download using classic FM GUI_DOWNLOAD
    CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
      EXPORTING
        filename                = lv_filename                     " Name of file
        filetype                = 'ASC'                " File Type (ASC or BIN)
        write_field_separator   = 'X'                " Separate Columns by Tabs in Case of ASCII Download
        confirm_overwrite       = 'X'                " Overwrite File Only After Confirmation
      TABLES
        data_tab                = it_data                     " Transfer table
      EXCEPTIONS
        file_write_error        = 1                    " Cannot write to file
        no_batch                = 2                    " Front-End Function Cannot Be Executed in Background
        gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3                    " Incorrect Front End
        invalid_type            = 4                    " Invalid value for parameter FILETYPE
        no_authority            = 5                    " No Download Authorization
        unknown_error           = 6
        header_not_allowed      = 7                    " Invalid header
        separator_not_allowed   = 8                    " Invalid separator
        filesize_not_allowed    = 9                    " Invalid file size
        header_too_long         = 10                   " The header information is limited to 1023 bytes at present
        dp_error_create         = 11                   " Cannot Create Data Provider
        dp_error_send           = 12                   " Error Sending Data with DataProvider
        dp_error_write          = 13                   " Error Writing Data with DataProvider
        unknown_dp_error        = 14                   " Error when calling data provider
        access_denied           = 15                   " Access to File Denied
        dp_out_of_memory        = 16                   " Not Enough Memory in DataProvider
        disk_full               = 17                   " Storage Medium Full
        dp_timeout              = 18                   " Timeout of DataProvider
        file_not_found          = 19                   " Could not find file
        dataprovider_exception  = 20                   " General Exception Error in Data Provider
        control_flush_error     = 21                   " Error in Control Framework
        OTHERS                  = 22.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      WRITE: / 'TXT file downloaded:', lv_filename.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* PDF File Downloader Implementation
*----------------------------------------------------------------------


CLASS zcl_pdf_downloader DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_file_downloader.  " Implements interface
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_pdf_downloader IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD zif_file_downloader~download_file.

    DATA: lv_filename TYPE string VALUE 'C:\temp\output4.pdf',
          lt_pdf      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF x255.

    " Generate PDF binary from given data using APOC PDF generator
    DATA(lo_pdf) = NEW cl_apoc_pdf_generator( ).
    lo_pdf->create_pdf(
      EXPORTING
        input             = it_data
      RECEIVING
        pdf_binary_output = DATA(rv_bin)
    ).

    " Convert XSTRING PDF to binary table
    CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
      EXPORTING
        buffer     = rv_bin
      TABLES
        binary_tab = lt_pdf.

    " Save as binary file on local machine
    cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download(
      EXPORTING
        filename                  = lv_filename                     " Name of file
        filetype                  = 'BIN'               " File type (ASCII, binary ...)
        confirm_overwrite         = 'X'                " Overwrite File Only After Confirmation
      CHANGING
        data_tab                  = lt_pdf                     " Transfer table
      EXCEPTIONS
        file_write_error          = 1                    " Cannot write to file
        no_batch                  = 2                    " Front-End Function Cannot Be Executed in Backgrnd
        gui_refuse_filetransfer   = 3                    " Incorrect Front End
        invalid_type              = 4                    " Invalid value for parameter FILETYPE
        no_authority              = 5                    " No Download Authorization
        unknown_error             = 6                    " Unknown error
        header_not_allowed        = 7                    " Invalid header
        separator_not_allowed     = 8                    " Invalid separator
        filesize_not_allowed      = 9                    " Invalid file size
        header_too_long           = 10                   " Header information currently restricted to 1023 bytes
        dp_error_create           = 11                   " Cannot create DataProvider
        dp_error_send             = 12                   " Error Sending Data with DataProvider
        dp_error_write            = 13                   " Error Writing Data with DataProvider
        unknown_dp_error          = 14                   " Error when calling data provider
        access_denied             = 15                   " Access to File Denied
        dp_out_of_memory          = 16                   " Not Enough Memory in DataProvider
        disk_full                 = 17                   " Storage Medium full
        dp_timeout                = 18                   " Timeout of DataProvider
        file_not_found            = 19                   " Could not find file
        dataprovider_exception    = 20                   " General Exception Error in DataProvider
        control_flush_error       = 21                   " Error in Control Framework
        not_supported_by_gui      = 22                   " GUI does not support this
        error_no_gui              = 23                   " GUI not available
        OTHERS                    = 24
    ).

    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      WRITE: / 'PDF file downloaded:', lv_filename.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* DOC File Downloader Implementation
*----------------------------------------------------------------------


CLASS zcl_doc_downloader DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_file_downloader.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_doc_downloader IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD zif_file_downloader~download_file.
    DATA: lv_filename TYPE string VALUE 'C:\temp\output.doc'.

    " Download using GUI_DOWNLOAD in ASCII mode
    CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
      EXPORTING
        filename                 = lv_filename                     " Name of file
        filetype                 = 'ASC'                " File Type (ASC or BIN)
        write_lf_after_last_line = abap_true            " Writes LF even after last line
        write_field_separator    = 'X'
        confirm_overwrite        = 'X'
      TABLES
        data_tab                 = it_data                     " Transfer table
      EXCEPTIONS
        file_write_error         = 1                    " Cannot write to file
        no_batch                 = 2                    " Front-End Function Cannot Be Executed in Background
        gui_refuse_filetransfer  = 3                    " Incorrect Front End
        invalid_type             = 4                    " Invalid value for parameter FILETYPE
        no_authority             = 5                    " No Download Authorization
        unknown_error            = 6
        header_not_allowed       = 7                    " Invalid header
        separator_not_allowed    = 8                    " Invalid separator
        filesize_not_allowed     = 9                    " Invalid file size
        header_too_long          = 10                   " The header information is limited to 1023 bytes at present
        dp_error_create          = 11                   " Cannot Create Data Provider
        dp_error_send            = 12                   " Error Sending Data with DataProvider
        dp_error_write           = 13                   " Error Writing Data with DataProvider
        unknown_dp_error         = 14                   " Error when calling data provider
        access_denied            = 15                   " Access to File Denied
        dp_out_of_memory         = 16                   " Not Enough Memory in DataProvider
        disk_full                = 17                   " Storage Medium Full
        dp_timeout               = 18                   " Timeout of DataProvider
        file_not_found           = 19                   " Could not find file
        dataprovider_exception   = 20                   " General Exception Error in Data Provider
        control_flush_error      = 21                   " Error in Control Framework
        OTHERS                   = 22.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      WRITE: / 'DOC file downloaded:', lv_filename.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* Factory Class - Returns Correct Downloader Object
*----------------------------------------------------------------------


CLASS zcl_file_factory DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_downloader
      IMPORTING iv_type              TYPE string
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_downloader) TYPE REF TO zif_file_downloader.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_file_factory IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_downloader.
    " Choose correct class based on file type
    CASE iv_type.
      WHEN 'TXT'.
        CREATE OBJECT ro_downloader TYPE zcl_txt_downloader.
      WHEN 'PDF'.
        CREATE OBJECT ro_downloader TYPE zcl_pdf_downloader.
      WHEN 'DOC'.
        CREATE OBJECT ro_downloader TYPE zcl_doc_downloader.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        MESSAGE 'Invalid format!' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

INITIALIZATION.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* Selection Screen
*----------------------------------------------------------------------

  " Choose format dynamically
  PARAMETERS: p_type TYPE string DEFAULT 'TXT'.  " Options: TXT / PDF / DOC

*----------------------------------------------------------------------
* Main Logic
*----------------------------------------------------------------------

START-OF-SELECTION.

  DATA: lo_down  TYPE REF TO zif_file_downloader.

  " Get some sample data from SFLIGHT table
  SELECT FROM sflight
    FIELDS * INTO TABLE (lt_data)
    UP TO 10 ROWS.

  " Use factory to get correct downloader object
  lo_down = zcl_file_factory=>get_downloader( iv_type = p_type ).
  lo_down->download_file( it_data = lt_data ).

For TEXT:-

 We can see in our folder.

For WORD--

We can see in our folder--

For PDF:-

We can see in our folder--

Conclusion:-

In this blog, we explored how the Factory Design Pattern can be effectively applied in ABAP to download data in different formats such as TEXT, WORD, PDF without tightly coupling the logic to a specific format. By introducing a factory class that decides which concrete formatter to instantiate, we achieve:

  • Flexibility – easily add new output formats in the future without modifying existing logic.
  • Reusability – the same factory and interface can be used across multiple applications.
  • Maintainability – reduces redundant code and keeps responsibilities clearly separated.
  • Scalability – supports evolving business needs where multiple consumers may require different file formats.

The Factory Design Pattern not only promotes clean code principles but also aligns with object-oriented best practices in ABAP. By leveraging this approach, developers can build solutions that are extensible, testable, and future-proof.

Thank You.

entry.title: Public Cloud Warehouse Management: Setup Email Notification for Error Posting
entry.content:
Background:

S/4HANA Public Cloud ERP has an embedded module to run the warehouse management functionalities (Cloud WM). Many postings in cloud WM (posting changes, difference postings, stock upload etc) are executed in a synchronous mode which posting errors will show immediately after the button click. However, for some postings in cloud WM (especially in the area of delivery posting like GR/GI, quantity adjust, stock attribute change from delivery processing etc), they are executed in an asynchronous way. There are pros and cons of processing in async and they will not be discussed here. Even there are many simulation checks added to avoid posting errors during execution, there are still possibilities that async posting fails with error and leaves an error in message queue. The error can be viewed in app “Warehouse Monitor”, node “Tools” -> Message Queue.

(the error above is manipulated)

The posting error will normally cause stock inconsistency between the cloud ERP side and cloud WM side. Additionally, the longer time it takes to solve the posting error, the more complex it will take to solve the issue. So it is essential to get notified of the posting error as early as possible and take action. To achieve this, customers can setup easily an email notification with below steps.

Setup Steps:

1. Assign the business user with a role which contains business catalog SAP_CA_BC_COM_CONF_PC, you can also assign the dependent BCs if the business user would also need to view and process the error message in AIF

2. The business user logon the system and open app “Assign Recipients to Users”, add the business user and assign the below namespace

3. Make sure in the upper right corner of the personalization, in the setting, the notification for the email is switched on.

4. Then when there is an error message, the business user shall receive a mail like below

entry.title: Extending SAP FSM: How I Solved a Real Service Manager Problem Using an External LLM
entry.content:
The Problem I Actually Faced on the Project

In one of my recent SAP FSM projects, I noticed something that really stayed with me.

When I visited my client’s office, I saw a Service Manager going through multiple service calls. He was opening each call, checking checklists, reading reports, and trying to understand what the technician had done.

Out of curiosity, I asked him about it.

He told me this is part of his daily routine every day he goes through all these service calls to decide what needs to be done next.

At that moment, I realized how much effort it actually takes from the business side.

  • He opened around 30–40 service calls, and for each one, he had to:
  • Open the activity
  • Go through checklists or attachments
  • Read long technician notes
  • Figure out what actually happened

After some time, he said:

“All the data is there… but it’s too much to read.”

That really hit me.

Because at that point, I felt like  at least I should be able to do something to make this easier for them.

That’s where this whole idea started.

Why I Went "Off-Road" with an External LLM

I know what you're thinking: "Doesn't SAP have native GenAI for this?" Yes, they do. And it's great for standard scenarios.

However, many of the clients I work with have specific security requirements or already pay for an Azure OpenAI or AWS Bedrock instance. They want flexibility. They want to tune the "personality" of the summary to match their specific industry whether it's Heavy Construction Equipments or medical devices. So, I built a side-by-side extension to prove we could get custom results directly in the FSM UI.

Look at a typical note a technician might enter into their tablet/mobile while standing at a hot customer site , often in a hurry and focused on getting the job done.

RAW TECHNICIAN NOTE — INSPECTION CARRIED: "Visited the site and reported motor overheating on 2nd floor pump room. Checked motor temp reading 94°C (max limit 75°C). Inspected cooling fan, blades partly blocked with debris and dust buildup. Cleaned fan blades as temporary fix. Checked ambient temp in room, high with no ventilation. Informed site supervisor about ventilation issue. Motor running temp dropped to 82°C after cleaning, still above normal. Added temporary warning tag on motor. Full motor replacement or rewinding required. Customer sign received, site engineer notified."

This is great info, but it's hard to scan. I wanted to turn that mess into a structured Work Performed / Issue / Next Step view without asking the technician to change how they type.

A note on data scope: 

While working on this, I also explored whether additional checklist inputs or structured fields could help improve the output further.

That approach is definitely possible.

But for this solution, I intentionally kept it simple.

How I Wired It Together (The Technical Setup)

While building this, I tried to keep the setup as simple and stable as possible. I didn’t want something too complex that might break with future updates in SAP Field Service Management.

So I focused on using standard capabilities and just extending them where needed. Here’s how the flow actually works.

How the Flow Works

1. Technician Input : 

Everything starts with the technician.

After completing the tasks, the technician fills out the checklist in the FSM mobile app and enters details in the “Inspection Carried” field (or any similar field being used).

There’s no strict format here ,  technicians write freely based on what they observe and do on-site. I didn’t want to change this behavior.

2. Trigger (Business Rule Execution) : 

Once the checklist is completed, a business rule is triggered automatically.

This was important for me — the whole process had to run in the background without adding any extra steps for the technician.

3. Sending Data to LLM & Processing the Response : 

This is where the main processing happens.

Using a webhook action in FSM, I send the technician’s notes to an external LLM through an HTTP POST request.

4. The Logic : 

The key part here is the prompt.

Instead of just asking the LLM to “summarize,” I guided it to behave like a technical reviewer — someone who understands service reports and formats them properly.

Here’s the exact structure I used:

{
"model": "your-model-name",
"messages": [
{
"role": "user",
"content": "You are a professional field service report assistant. Read the technician notes and return a structured summary in exactly this format: Work Performed: [what was done] Issue Identified: [what problem was found] Recommended Next Action: [follow-up needed, or write Not required] Rules: - Preserve all technical terms exactly - Fix grammar and clarity - Do not add information not in the original notes - If a section has no information, write 'Not mentioned' Technician Notes: {{InspectionCarried}}"
}
]
}

5. Storing the Summary Back in FSM :  

The generated summary is then written back into SAP FSM ,  typically in a UDF on the service call.

This ensures:

  • Original data remains unchanged
  • AI output is stored separately
  • Easy access for users

6. Service Manager View :

From a user perspective, this is where the real value shows up.

When the service manager opens the service call:

  • No need to open checklists
  • No need to go through long notes
  • No need to download reports

They can directly see a clean, structured summary on the same screen.

Before & After: The Transformation

Before: Unstructured NoteAfter: AI-Generated Summary
"Visited the site and reported motor overheating on 2nd floor pump room. Checked motor temp reading 94°C (max limit 75°C). Inspected cooling fan, blades partly blocked with debris and dust buildup. Cleaned fan blades as temporary fix. Checked ambient temp in room, high with no ventilation. Informed site supervisor about ventilation issue. Motor running temp dropped to 82°C after cleaning, still above normal. Added temporary warning tag on motor. Full motor replacement or rewinding required. Customer sign received, site engineer notified."Work Performed: Visited the site and investigated the reported motor overheating in the 2nd‑floor pump room. Measured motor temperature at 94 °C (maximum limit 75 °C). Inspected the cooling fan and found the blades partially blocked with debris and dust; cleaned the fan blades as a temporary fix. Checked the ambient temperature in the room, noted it was high with no ventilation, and informed the site supervisor of the ventilation issue. After cleaning, the motor’s running temperature dropped to 82 °C. Applied a temporary warning tag to the motor.

Issue Identified: Motor overheating caused by obstructed fan blades and inadequate ventilation, resulting in temperatures (94 °C initially, 82 °C after cleaning) that exceed the normal operating limit.

Recommended Next Action: Replace or rewind the motor (full motor replacement or rewinding required). 

What I Learned

The biggest win here isn't the "AI hype" - it's the consistency.

Whether you have a technician who writes ten pages or one who writes three words, the Service Manager now sees a standardized report. We aren't replacing the technician's input; we are just making it readable for the people making the big decisions.

In FSM, we often focus on "how to capture data." I think it’s time we start focusing more on "how to consume it."

entry.title: Re: Variable inheritance issue between classic and optimi...
entry.content:
Dear DavidMS,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

To address this scenario effectively, it is recommended reviewing the following points to ensure a resolution:

1. For the variable settings, please kindly first refer to help document "Save Story Without Mandatory Variables". This option "Clear Variables and Use Server-Defined Values" means that any variable value we set in story level would be cleared and variable values set at backend would be respected. Therefore, if it is desired to pass the variable value from the source story to the target story, we need to select the variable setting as "Save Current Variable Selections" in the target story. Otherwise, the variable values defined in the backend would be executed.

2. If this hyperlink is defined with External URL or defined via script and this variable parameter is defined in this URL, please kindly first refer to the information in help document Variable Parameters | SAP Help Portal. Please kindly check whether the format of this URL has followed the instruction in this help document. After defining this URL, please kindly also try accessing it in browser directly and check whether it can work as expected. 

3. As for the behavior difference when accessing this story in the current tab or new tab, it is deserved to check if this difference is due to the story Full Viewer Mode and story Lite Viewer mode. When accessing the target story in the current browser tab, the story full viewer mode would be enabled by default. When accessing the story in the new browser tab, if there is no unsupported features detected, the story lite viewer mode would be enabled automatically. Therefore, it is worth checking if the story URL API behavior may have some difference in these two story view modes. For more information related to the Lite Viewer Mode, please refer to help document Lite Viewer Usage Restrictions | SAP Help Portal.

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: SAP DataSphere – Moving Objects: Table, Dataflow from one Space to another Space.
entry.content:
During a recent implementation, an observation was made while moving objects between spaces in   After transporting a view from one space to another, field names were automatically converted to uppercase.

This post highlights the reason behind this behavior and its impact.

⸻

What Happened?
• A view was moved from a source space to a target space
• After deployment in the target space, all field names appeared in uppercase
• Previously, the field names were in mixed case (e.g., AssetNumber)

⸻

Why Does This Happen?


• Objects are not directly copied between spaces
• They are exported and re-imported into the target space
• During this process, objects are re-created and deployed again
• Metadata is regenerated, and system standardization is applied
• This results in technical field names being converted to uppercase

entry.title: Conditions Not Saved After First Entry in Purchase Contract
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to share an issue we encountered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where where pricing conditions are not saved after being entered for the first time in a purchase contract in the App Manage Purchase Contracts. This may help others facing similar inconsistencies in condition handling.

 

Issue:

In one of our test scenarios, condition DRV1 was maintained in a purchase contract.

The steps were as follows:

  1. Open the purchase contract in Manage Purchase Contracts.
  2. Navigate to the conditions section.
  3. Enter condition DRV1.
  4. Save the contract.

At first glance, everything seemed fine — no error messages were shown during the save process.
However, when checking the Purchase Contract Output, the condition was not printed here. And when reopening the purchase contract afterward, the condition DRV1 was no longer visible and appeared to be missing entirely.

Interestingly, when the same condition DRV1 was entered a second time, followed by saving the contract again, the condition is then stored correctly and remains visible when reopening the document.

 

Analysis:

At first, this behavior looked like a system defect or an issue with condition determination to us. Since there are no error messages and the save action completes successfully, the expectation was that the condition data has been stored.

However, the fact that the condition was only saved after re-entering it a second time suggests that the system never actually registered the initial input as confirmed data.

 

Root Cause:

The root cause of the issue is missing confirmation of the condition entry. SAP Support confirmed that the system was showing its' expected behaviour here.

When maintaining conditions, the entered condition must be explicitly confirmed by pressing Enter.
If the user enters the condition and immediately saves the document without pressing Enter, the system does not fully recognize the condition line as final input — even though it appears on the screen.

As a result, the condition is not transferred to the document data and is therefore not persisted during save.

 

Solution:

To ensure that conditions are saved correctly:

✅Confirm the condition with the Enter key before saving.

Once the condition has been confirmed with Enter, it will be saved correctly and will remain visible when the contract is reopened and will also be printed on the generated output.

 

Summary:

If you encounter situations where conditions or other values appear to be “lost” after saving, always check whether the input was properly confirmed beforehand by using the Enter key before saving.

entry.title: 2H 2025 First Release of SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll for USA
entry.content:
Dear all,

I hope you are doing well.

I am excited to announce the first release of SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP), based on SAP Human Capital Management for SAP S/4HANA, for the United States (US) via the Solution Builder.

This is especially exciting for those of you looking for an accelerated implementation for Employee Central Payroll for the US customers.

What does this package include? The package consists of four key building blocks:

  1. Gross-to-Net Payroll Core
  2. Benefit Processing Core
  3. Post-Payroll Activities
  4. Absence Processing Core

(The configurations and possible extensions, along with an explanation of logic, are presented in our workbook, and you can refer to it for more details.)

 

1.Gross to Net Payroll Core

The building block Gross to Net Payroll Core bundles blocks and related configurations to implement the replication of payroll specific master data from Employee Central Core to Employee Central Payroll that is needed to successfully run payroll for USA employees. The implementation content is aligned with the best practice configurations delivered for Employee Central Core.

Personnel Administration

This process covers the replication of employee master data created during hiring or updated as result of performing changes in the employee file, as well as the correctness check of that data.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Maintain Company details.
  • Maintain Enterprise and Personnel Structure
  • Create Work schedules, Pay Structure and Organizational Information
  • Maintain PTP integration settings for Personnel Administration
  • Capture all basic employee data in one single source of truth
  • Smart business process execution
  • Reduces errors in self-service and boosts adoption through innovations, such as built-in wizards, smart business rules, org charts, audit history, and flexible workflows

Payroll Administration

This process describes the payroll administration. The configuration needed for the successful payroll processing are maintained in this section.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Payroll Schema and rules corrections
  • Maintain payroll area and periods
  • Maintain Payroll Control Record
  • Execute Payroll Simulation Run
  • Release Payroll Area for Correction
  • Correct Payroll Relevant Master Data and / or Time Data
  • Trigger Employee Master Data Replication
  • Configure the payroll wage types
  • Overpayment recovery process
  • Maintain PTP integration setting for Payroll
  • Enables the payroll processing and generation of payroll results
  • Helps to have full traceability of an employee’s payroll processing

Garnishment

This process describes the garnishment processing. The configuration relevant for garnishment processing is maintained in this section.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Master data setup needed for Garnishment
  • Specific configuration for service charges and order types
  • Adjustments and garnishment processing
  • Enables the garnishment processing and generation of garnishment results on payroll processing

Tax

This process describes tax validation and generation. The configuration relevant for tax processing is maintained in this section.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Tax company assignment to organization
  • Specific configuration for tax models
  • Enables tax processing and tax deduction during payroll processing

Posting to Finance

This process describes the configuration needed for posting the payroll result to the finance system and complete the payment process.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Employee grouping and accounts creation
  • Wage Type configuration and posting period maintenance
  • Posting variant creations
  • Execute Financial Accounting Posting Simulation Run
  • Create Test Payment Data using Preliminary Program DME
  • Enables the posting of payroll result into the finance system
  • Create Payment Data using Preliminary Program DME

 

2.Benefits Processing Core

The building block Benefits Processing Core bundles system configurations that are needed for performing benefits processing for employees in USA. It also describes implementation content for benefits processing in payroll.

401K Plan

Key Features

Business Benefits

● Employee grouping and 401K plans configuration settings.

● Configure other plans for the employees.

● View Employee Contribution Information

● View Employer Contribution Information

● Enables the 401K plans processing for the employees

 

3.Post Payroll Activities

The building block Post Payroll Activities bundles system configuration needed for the successful posting of the payroll results post the live payroll processing.

Third-Party Remittance

This process describes the configuration needed for maintaining the third-party remittance settings needed for remittance processing.

Key Features

Business Benefits

  • Execute Third-Party Remittance Run
  • Execute Third-Party Remittance Posting Run
  • Automatic calculation of remittance.

 

4.Absence Processing Core

This process describes the replication of absence data. After the time-off request has been approved, the absence period of the employee is marked as certain in the Employee Central system, and the time data is replicated automatically to Employee Central Payroll, where it can be checked for correctness.

View in Employee Central the replication status for each employee to see if there are any failed jobs. Use the Display or Overview function to view for the employee the absence data as transferred from Employee Central to Employee Central Payroll.

Absence Administration

Key Features

Business Benefits

● Trigger Employee Time Off Replication

● Update Employee Absence Record in Employee Central Payroll

● Initiate / Receive Employee Time Off Replication Confirmation Message

● Monitor Employee Time Off Replication

● View Employee Absence Data

● Enables employees to register their Leave of Absence at the period for which they are absent

● Helps to have full traceability when an employee takes a leave of absence and returns from the leave

 

These best practices add significant value by accelerating your implementations, and I hope to see increased adoption from you (partners) in the coming days.

I wish you the best of luck with your implementations.

If you have any feedback, please leave a comment below.

 

Thanks & best regards,

Sreenivasan Ravi

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud: Professional Services Customer Proj...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there is no delivered standard functionality to split “List Price” and “Discount” into separate accounting lines at invoice level. By default, condition types like PSP0/PCP0 influence planned revenue only, not the billed revenue. To achieve the business requirement, you need to use extensibility either create custom condition types mapped to revenue and discount accounts, or leverage the Cloud Application Studio / Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for accounting determination.

If accounting-level separation is mandatory, implement custom condition types with separate GL accounts. If reporting-only is acceptable, use Group Reporting derivation rules. Meanwhile, monitor SAP release notes for native support of discount postings.

entry.title: Sharing SAP S/4HANA Data with Databricks Using BDC Connect and Delta Sharing
entry.content:
In our scenario, data originates from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, is replicated into SAP Datasphere, and then securely shared to Databricks using SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Connect and Delta Sharing. This approach enables zero‑copy data access in Databricks while keeping data governed and managed within SAP.

This blog focuses on the end‑to‑end data replication and sharing flow, along with key prerequisites, monitoring steps, and performance considerations.

High Level Flow:

S/4HANA - >  Replication Flow (CDS-based) -> Datasphere Object Store (Parquet files) -> Managed Data Product -> BDC Connect -> Delta Sharing -> Databricks
 
At a high level:
  • SAP S/4HANA replicates data using released CDS views
  • Data is stored in Datasphere Object Store as Parquet files
  • A managed data product is created on top of this data
  • The data product is shared externally using BDC Connect
  • Databricks consumes the shared data via Delta Sharing

Data Replication from S/4HANA to SAP Business Data Cloud:

The ABAP Data Integration framework—specifically the ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS)—is used to replicate data from S/4HANA into SAP Business Data Cloud.

Key points:

  • Only released CDS views can be replicated
  • The available CDS views depend on:
    • Your S/4HANA release
    • The data products shipped with that release
  • Replication supports:
    • Initial load
    • Change Data Capture (CDC) for deltas

Pre-Requisites:

Platform & Connectivity

The following setup steps are mandatory but not covered in detail in this blog:

  • Enable BDC Cockpit
  • Establish BDC Connect between SAP BDC and Databricks
  • Create a Formation in SAP for Me Marketplace
  • Ensure Datasphere Object Store is available and configured (Delta Sharing does not work with HANA tables)
SAP S/4HANA Prerequisites:

Before starting replication, review the SAP prerequisite note for your S/4HANA version:

  • SAP Note: 3500131 – ABAP Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud

ABAP Integration is available starting from the following releases (subject to change, always verify the note):

  • SAP S/4HANA 2021
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 07
  • SAP S/4HANA 2022
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 05
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023
    • TCI 3581682 implemented or SPS 03
  • SAP S/4HANA 2025
    • Initial release
  • The replication framework must be active and CDC jobs must be healthy and running.  Important SAP Note 3561739 - "ABAP Data Integration - Technical Jobs" and set jobs in scope. 

Required Jobs in SJOBREPO:

After configuration, verify that the following jobs are in scope and active (green status) in transaction SJOBREPO. Activation may take some time.

Technical Job Definition Description
/LTB/JOB_DISPATCHERCentral cross‑application job scheduler
SAP_DHAFR_WRITER_START_DAEMONMonitors delta replication queue and triggers delta replication
SAP_DHBAS_RETENTION_JOBExecutes data retention actions
SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMONTriggers initial load and calculation jobs
SAP_DHCDC_DISPATCHERCentral dispatcher for CDC jobs
SAP_DHCDC_HOUSEKEEPERNightly housekeeping job

Note:
SAP_DHCDC_HOUSEKEEPER is automatically added when replication starts and removed when replication is not used.

Activating Data Packages in BDC Cockpit

  1. Log in to BDC Cockpit
  2. Navigate to Intelligent Applications and Data Packages
  3. Verify that the required applications are part of your formation
  4. Select the relevant data packages and click Activate

Activation duration depends on:

  • Data volume
  • Number of CDS views
  • Available parallel jobs

BDC Cockpit Screenshot:

You should see the relevant intelligent apps and data packages for the applications included in your formation. Based on your requirement and your S4HANA version, you can activate the relevant data packages.
You should be able to see the Activate button on the top right corner

The activation takes time depending on the amount of data to be transferred, some performance techniques are listed at the bottom of this blog.

Monitoring and Troubleshooting:

You can monitor the for any error logs using DHCDCMON tcode > application logs, there are chances that you might run into errors as mentioned in this note 0003610376    Cannot start ACP daemon (client XXX) - ABAP CDC Engine.

Ensure that the R_JR_BTCJOBS_GENERATOR job has been running recently for the client (check for this job in active/finished status). This job should run every hour for each active client.

Check whether the SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMON job is stuck in 'released/scheduled' status. If so, and the R_JR_BTCJOBS_GENERATOR job has been running, delete this instance of the SAP_DHCDC_RT_START_ACP_DAEMON job before waiting up to an hour for it to be run (job may need to be deactivated and reactivated in SJOBREPO after deleting its stuck instance in SM37).

Data Package Activation Status

After activation starts or completes:

  • Status is visible in BDC Cockpit
  • Data products that require a higher minimum S/4HANA version will fail activation and are clearly marked
 

 

Performance Considerations:

Initial Load Parallelization

  • Initial load of CDS views is parallelized by default
  • Parallelism depends on job configuration in transaction DHADM

Relevant SAP Note:

  • 3223735 – Control of parallelism
    • Parameters: TRANSFER_MIN_JOBS / TRANSFER_MAX_JOBS
    • Job: /1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA

Delta Loads

  • Delta (CDC) loads are not parallelized
  • They run continuously based on data changes

Final Step:

To delta share the data products to databricks, navigate to Catalog and Marketplace in your BDC cockpit and you should be able to see the active data products, select the data product.

Click Share icon:

Add Target and choose the BDC connect details to Databricks and share

Databricks Consumption:

Once shared:

  • Databricks users can see the data product in their catalog
  • They can mount or query the shared dataset using Delta Sharing
  • No physical data copy is required
  • Data scientists can immediately perform analytics and ML workloads

Conclusion

By combining SAP S/4HANA replication, SAP Business Data Cloud managed data products, and BDC Connect with Delta Sharing, organizations can enable secure, governed, zero‑copy analytics in Databricks. This architecture minimizes data duplication, reduces operational cost, and ensures SAP remains the system of governance while enabling advanced analytics and machine learning.

entry.title: Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 Fiori app - Restrict Create/Edit/Display based on Authorization Role
entry.content:
Hi,

entry.title: Re: Business Area (GSBER) not updated after Asset change...
entry.content: Yes, this is standard SAP behavior. Once the Business Area (GSBER) is derived during PR creation, it is not automatically re-derived if the Asset (ANLN1) is changed later. There is no delivered configuration to force re-derivation. SAP recommends using a BAdI such as ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (which you also indicated) if you need dynamic updates.

entry.title: Re: Event-Based Revenue Recognition - How to consider tax...
entry.content:
SAP deliberately excludes tax postings from EBRR accruals to remain compliant with legal requirements. Taxes are only posted at the time of billing. There is no standard setting to force VAT postings during revenue recognition accruals. Even if you try to extend account determination, the system will not generate tax lines because the tax procedure is tied to billing documents, not accrual postings.

My advice is to treat this as a compliance safeguard rather than a missing configuration. If your business insists on seeing tax impact earlier, the safest path is to build a reporting simulation rather than altering FI postings.

entry.title: Re: SAP FI Asset accounting treatment with CO WBS AUC
entry.content:
Your two proposed options are both valid within SAP’s standard framework, and the way you’ve structured them is consistent with how SAP expects WBS-driven investment flows to work.

  • Option 1 (Standard PS Process): Best for global consistency, audit traceability, and detailed project reporting.
  • Option 2 (Lightweight): Best for operational efficiency in entities that don’t need deep project-level analysis, but carries risk of reduced transparency.

So the recommended approach can be

If global harmonization and audit traceability are top priorities → go with Option 1.

If operational simplicity for U.S. entity is critical and project-level analysis is less important → Option 2 can work, but you must document the rationale and ensure compliance with accounting standards.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC 6.0 – Fiscal Year Variant Change ( 5-4-4 → Ca...
entry.content:
Modifying an existing year‑dependent Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) in ECC with historical postings is considered high‑risk and generally not recommended. The safer practice is to create a new FYV aligned to calendar months and migrate balances during the S/4HANA conversion. SAP does not provide a supported way to “re‑open” past periods (like 27–30 June) without redefining the FYV, and audit/governance teams usually prefer a clean cutover rather than retroactive changes.

Though I am well aware of your comments "We are looking for real‑world ECC experience, not just OB29 theory.", below are the key risks of modifying existing FYV

1) Historical Postings:
Changing OB29 for a year‑dependent FYV affects all prior years. Reports, reconciliations, and asset histories may misalign.

2) Integration Impact:
FI, CO, MM, SD, RE‑FX, Asset Accounting, BW, and Payroll postings all reference fiscal periods. A change can break reconciliation across modules.

3) Audit Concerns:
Retroactive changes to fiscal calendars are difficult to defend. Auditors typically expect continuity of past reporting.

4) Technical Limitations:
SAP does not support “in‑place” modification of FYV with history; OB29 changes are meant for new fiscal years only.

Check the below comparison of options

entry.title: Support Needed: OAuth2 SAML Bearer Destination connection to Successfactor Issue in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Dear Connections,

I’m reaching out regarding an issue we are currently facing with the OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion destination setup in SAP BTP for establishing a connection with SuccessFactors.

Background

We are working on integrating our CAP-based application deployed on SAP BTP with SuccessFactors using a destination configured with OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion authentication.

Current Status

  • The destination works successfully with Basic Authentication
  • However, when switching to OAuth2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion, the connection fails

Error Details

  • We are encountering the error:
    “No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'.","stacktrace":["Error: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion'."”

What We Have Verified

  • Destination is correctly configured in BTP
  • OAuth client is created in SuccessFactors
  • JWT token is available and being passed from CAP (via request headers)
  • User attributes (logonName) are available in the request context
  • CAP application is correctly bound to XSUAA and Destination services

Suspected Areas

  • OAuth configuration mismatch 
  • Certificate/trust configuration between BTP and SuccessFactors
  • SAML assertion generation issues via XSUAA

Request

Since this setup involves multiple components (CAP, XSUAA, Destination service, and SuccessFactors), I wanted to check if anyone have experience with similar OAuth2 SAML Bearer configurations in BTP.

It would be really helpful if you could:

  • Suggest any checks or troubleshooting steps we might be missing

CAPM Code:

const { executeHttpRequest } = require("@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client");
const { getDestination } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity');
module.exports = async function (request) {
         let payload = request.data;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Payload: " + JSON.stringify(payload));
        const workflowPath = `odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true`;
        oLogger.info("Workflow Path: " + workflowPath);
        payload["__metadata"] = { "uri": "Position" };
const destination = await getDestination({
        destinationName: "sfAdminDestination",
        userJwt
        });
const workflowRes = await executeHttpRequest(
            destination,
            {
                method: "POST",
                url: "odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true",
                data: payload,
                headers: {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
                    "Accept": "application/json"
                }
            }
        );

}
Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story: How to send notification alert via email w...
entry.content:
@S0019834417 

You could explore usage of ValueChangeOverThreshold api and isSendEmail API of class NotificationOptions to achieve this.

https://help.sap.com/doc/1639cb9ccaa54b2592224df577abe822/2026.2/en-US/index.html#DataChangeInsightType_P_static_ValueChangeOverThreshold

https://help.sap.com/doc/1639cb9ccaa54b2592224df577abe822/2026.2/en-US/index.html#NotificationOptions_PisSendEmail

Alternatively you could put the logic of your red threshold explicitly in the script. Something like below.

// 1. Identify recipients (SAC User IDs)
var receivers = ["USER_ID_1", "USER_ID_2"];
var resultSet = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet();
var thresholdBreached = false;

for (var i = 0; i < resultSet.length; i++) {
    var val = parseFloat(resultSet[i]["@MeasureName"].formattedValue);
    if (val < 70000) { // Your red threshold condition explicitly defined
        thresholdBreached = true;
        break;
    }
}

if (thresholdBreached) {
    Application.sendNotification({
        "title": "Threshold Breach Detected",
        "content": "A value has entered the RED zone. Please check the dashboard.",
        "receivers": receivers,,
        "isSendEmail": true
    });
}

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: PRODUCTION ORDERS MUST BE SETTLED AGAIN TWO MONTHS AF...
entry.content:
Here is the breakdown which would clear your doubts

Moving average price (V):
Every goods movement (GR, GI, settlement) can potentially change the material’s valuation. If settlement is missed and consumption happens, the system posts variances directly to P&L instead of adjusting the material value. That’s why you saw the costs bypass inventory and hit expense accounts.

Late settlement issue:
When users forget to settle, the production order variances are not capitalized into the material. Once consumption has already occurred, the system cannot retroactively adjust the moving average price for those past postings. It only posts the variance to P&L.

MR21 price change:
MR21 directly changes the material price in accounting. When the user changed the price on 31/03/2026, SAP reopened the possibility for settlement because the valuation basis had changed. The system then allowed the production order to be settled again, but instead of adjusting inventory (since consumption had already happened), it posted the difference to P&L. That’s why you saw the accounting document generated at that later date.

Why this happens:
With moving average price, SAP expects settlement to happen promptly after technical completion. If settlement is delayed and inventory has already been consumed, the link between production variances and inventory valuation is broken. MR21 essentially reset the valuation, which made the system reprocess the settlement, but by then the only option was to push the difference into profit and loss.

Having said that, the unusual behavior is not a bug but a consequence of using price control V with delayed settlements. MR21 changes the valuation layer, which re-enables settlement, but since the manufactured stock was already consumed, SAP cannot adjust inventory retrospectively and posts to P&L instead.

If your client wants to avoid this, the recommended practice is to use standard price (S) for manufactured items. That way, variances are always posted to price difference accounts, and you don’t run into these timing issues with moving average price.

entry.title: Re: change in Input variables which i am taking from CDS...
entry.content:
@kandagatla-nagaraju 

You could use a Date input control of type range with system Date calendar.

  1. Create Input Control: In the Builder panel of a chart or table, add a filter on your Date dimension.
  2. Set Range: Select Filter by Range.
  3. Define Current Date: In the "Current Date" dropdown, select Create Current Date Input Control.
  4. Configure:
    • Name: Give it a title (e.g., "Select Current Date").
    • Default: Keep it set to System Date / Period to ensure it always defaults to today's actual date when the story is opened.
    • Granularity: Choose "Day" to enable a daily calendar picker

It should create something like below.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Financial Closing cockpit (no?) value added for u...
entry.content:
Why This Happens
  • Design Philosophy: FCLOCO was built to enforce governance and auditability, not flexibility. It prioritizes compliance and control over user experience.
  • Templates: Task list templates are structured but not intuitive. Customization requires technical effort and often feels restrictive.
  • Tracking: Even if tasks are executed in SAP, FCLOCO doesn’t automatically update completion status unless integrated properly. This leads to manual updates.
  • Reporting: Reporting is limited. There is no global dashboard view unless you build custom CDS views or embed analytics.

 

Given below are Good Practices which you can consider
 
Automate Task Completion:
Link FCLOCO tasks directly to SAP transactions/programs so completion updates automatically.
Example: Assign the program for foreign currency valuation or depreciation run to the task—once executed, FCLOCO marks it complete.
 
Use Hierarchical Task Lists:
Organize tasks by country, company code, or process area. This makes navigation easier and reduces clutter.
 
Enhance Reporting:
Build custom SAP Fiori apps or CDS views to visualize closing status globally.
Few clients integrate FCLOCO with SAP Analytics Cloud for dashboards showing completion by country, overdue tasks, and bottlenecks.
 
Limit Administration:
Avoid overloading FCLOCO with micro-tasks. Group small steps into larger milestones to reduce administrative burden.
 
Change Management:
Train users on why FCLOCO matters (audit trail, compliance, global visibility). Without this, they see it as “extra work.”
 
Position FCLOCO as a control tool, not a replacement for Excel. Many companies still use Excel for local tracking but rely on FCLOCO for audit and global oversight.
 
So the bottom line message is you are not wrongly using FCLOCO; your experience is widely shared. The key is to automate task completion, reduce template complexity, and build reporting layers on top. FCLOCO is best treated as a compliance cockpit, while Excel or BI tools remain the “friendly” operational trackers.

entry.content:

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: I need to extract the AIM Configuration Filter Parame...
entry.content:
Hello Shruthi,

In case of Technical systems, the monitoring filter parameters are saved in the table /IMA/COLLCONFIG.
You can specify the category with 'CATEGORY_ID', e.g. IDOC, WS, PIAB_MBMSG, etc.
You can check the LMDBID of Technical systems referring to the KBA 3647048.

In case of Cloud systems, the monitoring filter parameters are saved in the table /IMA/CLD_FILTERS.
You can specify the monitoring use case with 'CATEGORY_KEY', e.g. SAP_ARIBA_CIG_MESSAGE, HCI, etc.
LMDBID of Cloud systems can be checked from the table /IMA/CLD_CONFIGS or /IMA/CLDPUSH_CFG(push based services).

Hope above helps you.
Best regards,
Claudia

entry.title: Re: AI-Assisted Ratings for Inferred Skills
entry.content:
Hi @RobertaP 

The "Assisted Ratings for Inferred Skills" feature enables AI-assisted ratings specifically for inferred skills. This feature infers skill ratings based on the titles and descriptions of learning items and programs, matching them to the appropriate proficiency levels in the Attributes Library. The AI-generated skill ratings are displayed on the Assisted Skills Inference page, where you can review the inferred skills and their ratings and then associate them with relevant items and programs. Once linked, these skills and ratings appear in the Attributes section of the Items and Programs pages. Basically, this enhancement improves skill inference by automatically identifying both skills and their ratings, eliminating the need for manual entry.

entry.title: Re: handle dynamic field control
entry.content:
Hi 

I don't know a good method, so this is one of those no-answer answers. My suggest is to maybe make multiple dynpros instead of one dynpro handling multiple scenarios with  dynamic field control.

I have some programs where it has become very complex with a lot of rules for single fields and also for fields inside table controls.
The reason I ended in this complex situation is classic. The business requested a program to handle something for production of -  lets say bikes. "We must be able register how much plastic is used in the handlebars, saddle and 2 wheels".

They are happy and use the program for 2 years. Then they come back with an idea "we also want to use this program for kick scooters without a saddle, so to prevent errors you must hide this field when it is kick scooters".

Next year they want to use the program for go-karts, so now we need four fields for registering wheels data, b only when it is go-karts.
Next they want to register both plastic and steel, but only for bikes and for green kick scooters.
..and so on.

From the beginning and with the first new requirements to the program it seemed to be an easy fix to use dynamic field control, but in the end it turned out to be very complex to maintain. 
If I from the beginning had know the full requirements, I would have made one dynpro for bikes, one for saddles and one for kick scooters. The rest of the program with pai/pbo moduls, sub routions, class methods etc. I would have structured with reusable objects, so I would for example only have one plastic registration mechanism reused in all 3 dynpros.

So, I'm sorry this answer is not good ideas how to make dynamic field control simple. It is an advice to only use dynamic field control in simple scenarios.

I am excited to follow this thread to see if others have truly good advice about how to handle complex dynamic field control.

Best regards 
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire about the account determination logic for subcontracting goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Background

We are currently using subcontracting processes for both:

  • Finished Goods (FERT)
  • Semi-Finished Goods (HALB)

The business requirement is to separate G/L accounts for:

  • Goods receipt (inventory offset account)
  • Component consumption

We have maintained Automatic Account Determination (GBB) with different:

  • General Modifications (ZOB, VBO)
  • Valuation Classes (e.g., 7900 for HALB, 7920 for FERT)

2. Observations

Based on our testing:

  1. For normal goods receipt (e.g., movement type 501):
    → GBB (ZOB) is applied and G/L accounts can be controlled
  2. For component consumption:
    → GBB (VBO) is applied and G/L accounts can be changed successfully
  3. For subcontracting goods receipt:
    → The inventory offset account is always posted to G/L 50300000
    → Changes in GBB settings (ZOB / VBO) are not reflected
    → Only the consumption part follows VBO settings

3. Questions

We would like to clarify the following:

  1. What is the standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt?
  2. Is the G/L account (e.g., 50300000) determined by:
    • OBYC / GBB configuration, or
    • Other internal logic (e.g., subcontracting or valuation-related logic)?
  3. Is there any configuration (e.g., SSCUI) to control or override this account?
  4. Is this behavior standard in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

4. Expectation

We would like to confirm whether it is possible to:

  • Assign different G/L accounts for subcontracting goods receipt
    (e.g., separate accounts for FERT and HALB)

If not, we would appreciate clarification on:

  • Standard design limitations
  • Recommended alternatives

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Michi
